english: gram (gram) lattice in the side of it, and gur-lcám (gur lcam) stakes supporting the roof Sch., - peculiar expressions relating to the felt-tents of the Mongol nomads.
1. saffron, Crocus Glr., Lt. - 2. marigold, Calendula, and similar yellow flowers C.
Ld. a small churn used for preparing tea.
a perforated skin, a hide full of holes Sch.

sbst. respect, reverence, devotion; also adj respectful, devout; dge-dún-la gús-pas þyag tsál-lo (dge dun la gus pas phyag tshal lo) the priesthood I respect with devotion; ma-gús-pa (ma gus pa) unbelieving, undevout Thgy.; *gus-záb čò-če* (*gus zab co ce*) W. to show a respectful willingness to serve; humble, gús-par gyúr-ba (gus par gyur ba) Cs.: 'to humble one's self'; in modern letters = pran. your most humble servant.
a kerchief for the head hanging down behind.

1. Cs. n. of a flower, Lt. and elsewhere, prob. = केसर; it is said to grow in Nepal, and to be called also pád-ma ge-sár (pad ma ge sar). - 2. Sch.: pistil, but, like ze-brú (ze bru), it signifies undoubtedly the organs of fructification in general, as the natural science of Tibet is certainly not acquainted with the sexual difference in the parts of flowers; ge-sár-ċan (ge sar can) the lotos flower Sch. - 3. n. of a fabulous king in the north of Tibet, with the epithet dmág-gi-rgyál-po (dmag gi rgyal po) Glr. and elsewh.; ge-sár-gyi sgruṅ (ge sar gyi sgrung) the fabulous history of the same.

hindrance, impediment, obstacle, gégs-med-par (gegs med par) without hindrance, unimpeded, te-tsóm daṅ gégs sél-ba (the tshom dang gegs sel ba) to remove doubts and hindrances Mil.; gegs-byéd byegs (gegs byed bgegs) a malignant spirit, causing impediments or mischief Zam.; čos-mdzád yóṅs-la gegs byéd-pa (chos mdzad yongs la gegs byed pa) to throw obstacles in the way of all pious people Pth.; saṅs-rgyás mi tób-pai gegs bži (sangs rgyas mi thob pai gegs bzhi) four obstacles to attaining the Buddhaship Thgy.; also without a negation: tób-pai grogs gró-am gégs-su gro (thob pai grogs gro am gegs su gro) will you help me or hinder me in obtaining...? Mil.; grúb-pai gegs (grub pai gegs) impediment to perfection.
branch of a tree, šiṅ-gel-pa (shing gel pa).
1. numerical sign for 123. - 2. num. inst. of dgú-bču (dgu bcu), in the abbreviated numbers go-gčig (go gcig) etc., 91-99 - 3 for gó-ča (go cha). - 4. for gó-bo (go bo).

1. place, room, space (prob. = gu (gu)); in this sense it is used in go-mtsams-méd-par (go mtshams med par) without intermediate spaces, continuous; bru sna tsogs go-mtsams-méd-par skyes (bru sna tshogs go mtshams med par skyes) grain of every kind grew densely, luxuriantly; go-mtsams-méd-par gáṅ-ba (go mtshams med par gang ba) closely filled Tar. 13; prob. also in go-čód (go chod): 'the space is cut off, or filled i e. the matter is done with, settled, satisfaction has been made; col. also: I have got enough, I am full, (the thing lost or missed) has been found, restored; *gho čò' soṅ* (*gho cho' song*) or *jhuṅ* (*jhung*) C., *go čód-kaṅ yoṅ* (*go chod kang yong*) W. he has managed the business well, he has executed his commission satisfactorily; des rgyál-bai gó mi čod (des rgyal bai go mi chod) by this the victory has not yet been fully decided Mil.; tos bsam sgom gsúm-gyi go čod (thos bsam sgom gsum gyi go chod) (by only once looking at the Ommanipadmehūm) every other hearing, thinking, or looking at is done away with, any thing further is rendered unnecessary Glr.; kyéd-la go mi čód-pai čos (khyed la go mi chod pai chos) a doctrine not satisfactory to you Mil.; bu tsab ṅa spyugs či þyir go mi čod (bu tshab nga spyugs ci phyir go mi chod) why should it not be sufficient that I be condemned to exile instead of my son? Pth. - 2. the proper place of a person or thing among other persons or things, position, rank, condition of life, so in many of the following compounds, the word being seldom used alone: þai gór du (phai gor du) the place, office, dignity of his father Dzl.; gó-nas (go nas) according to, in proportion to Glr.; go rgás-na (go rgas na) when rank and
